data.narrative:
Pixxel is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $95M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies across the sector, with three direct Series B comparables identified: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two triangulation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-progression methodology, using the $95M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $180M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-market anchoring via the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S multiple of 49.8x cannot be applied; Pixxel's revenue is not disclosed.

The two applicable methods support a triangulated range of $153M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $290M. This wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty in early-stage space company valuations, driven by heterogeneous capital requirements, commercial maturity, and business model differences among peers. The peer-median result notably exceeds the stage-progression estimate, suggesting either elevated investor appetite for this sector or material differentiation among the comparable set. Revenue visibility and pathway to profitability remain critical to narrowing this band.
